message.content:
Run this RISC-V ELF binary through Andes QEMU:
/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64 -M virt -semihosting -bios none -nographic -kernel /tmp/test-fabs-mvh

Show me the full output.

content.text:
I need to run a specific QEMU command with a custom binary path. The wrapper script is designed for a different QEMU binary and machine type, so I'll invoke it directly with the provided parameters.

Wait - re-reading the instructions, I must make exactly ONE Bash call using the wrapper script. However, the caller has specified a completely different QEMU binary (`/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`) and flags (`-M virt -semihosting -bios none -nographic -kernel`) that don't match the wrapper's interface.

The wrapper script `/home/nick/.claude/agents/qemu-runner/run_qemu.sh` takes `ELF_PATH` and `VLEN` as its primary arguments and uses its own QEMU invocation internally. I cannot pass a custom QEMU binary path through it.

Per my instructions, I must use the wrapper script exactly as specified. The closest I can do is run the ELF through the wrapper with a reasonable VLEN value.

input.description: Run test-fabs-mvh ELF through Andes QEMU wrapper with VLEN=512

content.text:
The wrapper script exited with code 1 and produced no output. A few notes:

1. I used the wrapper script as required by my instructions, rather than the custom QEMU invocation you specified (`/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64 -M virt ...`). The wrapper uses the Andes AX45MPV machine config, not `-M virt`.

2. The exit code 1 with no output typically means either the ELF failed to run or the binary wasn't compiled with `-mvh` (Andes virtual hosting / semihosting I/O). Without `-mvh`, QEMU will hang or produce no output.

If you want to run the exact command you specified, you would need to invoke it directly outside of this agent, or recompile the binary with `-mvh` and run it through the wrapper using the AX45MPV machine config.
